message:
frankentorch-0tw: wire sparse backward path in IndexSelect

- Add sparse: bool to TensorNodeOp::IndexSelect.
- Expose index_select_sparse on the autograd tape and
  tensor_index_select_sparse on FrankenTorchSession.
- In backward, when an IndexSelect is marked sparse and dim=0, build a
  SparseCOOTensor (sparse_dim=1) from the resulting dense gradient and
  surface it via TensorBackwardReport::sparse_gradient.
- Embedding::forward now routes through the sparse variant when
  with_options(sparse=true) was used; SparseAdam consequently updates
  only touched rows for sparse embedding tables.
- Tests:
  - ft-nn: embedding_sparse_backward_emits_sparse_grad,
    embedding_dense_backward_omits_sparse_grad
  - ft-optim: sparse_adam_with_sparse_embedding_only_updates_touched_rows,
    sparse_adam_dense_embedding_still_works
- ft-conformance test custom_function_layer_trains_end_to_end_with_adam
  is a pre-existing flake on main, unrelated to this change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"frankentorch-0tw","title":"Add sparse tensor storage and basic sparse ops","description":"## Progress

### Completed
1. SparseCOOTensor in ft-core with indices [sparse_dim, nnz], values [nnz, *dense_dims], coalesced flag
2. SparseCSRTensor in ft-core with crow_indices, col_indices, values
3. SparseTensorError enum with comprehensive validation errors
4. AutogradError::SparseTensor variant for error propagation
5. ft-api sparse tensor methods:
   - sparse_coo_tensor(coords, values, shape, dtype)
   - sparse_coo_tensor_from_indices(indices, values, shape, sparse_dim, coalesced)
   - sparse_csr_tensor(crow_indices, col_indices, values, shape)
   - tensor_to_sparse_coo(input) - dense to sparse conversion
   - sparse_coo_to_dense / sparse_csr_to_dense
6. ft-kernel-cpu sparse operations:
   - sparse_coo_matmul_dense_f64 - sparse @ dense matmul
   - sparse_coo_coalesce - merge duplicate indices
   - sparse_coo_add - element-wise addition
7. GradientValue enum (Dense/Sparse) in ft-autograd for unified gradient representation
8. sparse_gradients field in TensorBackwardReport for sparse gradient storage
9. Embedding.sparse field in ft-nn with with_options(sparse) constructor
10. SparseAdam now checks report for sparse gradients and iterates efficiently over COO entries

### Tests (11 core sparse + 177 optim + 299 core/kernel passing)
- 8 in ft-core: COO/CSR creation, to_dense roundtrip, error handling
- 3 in ft-kernel-cpu: matmul, coalesce, add
- All existing tests pass with new sparse gradient infrastructure

### Remaining for full sparse gradient flow
- Wire sparse backward path in IndexSelect when source tensor is marked sparse
- Test sparse gradient round-trip with Embedding + SparseAdam

 {"id":"frankentorch-1le","title":"Investigate persistent beads SQLite integrity warning after clean rebuild","description":"## Context
After a full non-destructive rebuild of `.beads/beads.db` from canonical `issues.jsonl`, `br doctor --json` still reports:

`sqlite.integrity_check: database disk image is malformed: page 17 is never used`

This persists even on the freshly rebuilt DB, while all operational checks are otherwise healthy (`br ready --json` is empty, sync metadata matches, blocked cache is not stale).

## Goal
Determine whether this warning reflects:
1. a genuine low-level SQLite file/layout problem,
2. expected frankensqlite behavior that `br doctor` should not flag as suspicious, or
3. a beads/br bug in the integrity-check path.

## Acceptance
- Reproduce the warning on the current canonical DB
- Compare against a freshly rebuilt temp DB
- Gather enough evidence to classify the issue as workspace-local vs tool-level
- If it is tool-level or not safely fixable from this repo, document the finding clearly and close or hand off with evidence","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:56:40.262095265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:00:06.096361407Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T14:00:06.096186135Z","close_reason":"Reproduced the integrity warning on canonical and rebuilt DBs, proved a plain SQLite VACUUM clears it, then observed br doctor --repair reintroduce the warning on a different page number while keeping logical metadata healthy; this classifies the issue as a tool-level beads/frankensqlite write-path problem rather than repo-local issue-data corruption","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-1nn","title":"Enforce index_add/index_copy shape + rank rules","description":"Audit: tensor_index_add/index_copy accept mismatched src/index shapes and non-1D indices; they silently ignore extra indices. Add tests for mismatch errors and f32 parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T12:57:29.258566505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T13:01:14.230978644Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T13:01:14.230629796Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-iey","title":"Add ONNX model export support","description":"ONNX export allows FrankenTorch models to be deployed in any ONNX-compatible runtime (ONNX Runtime, TensorRT, CoreML, etc.). This is important for production deployment and interoperability.

Implementation plan:
1. Create a new crate ft-onnx (or module in ft-serialize) that:
   a. Traces a model forward pass to capture the computation graph
   b. Maps FrankenTorch ops to ONNX operator set (opset 17+)
   c. Serializes the graph + weights to ONNX protobuf format
2. Add session.export_onnx(model, dummy_input, path) to ft-api
3. Map core ops: Linear, Conv2d, ReLU, BatchNorm, LSTM, Softmax, MatMul, Add, etc.
4. Use the onnx-pb crate for protobuf serialization (or manual protobuf encoding)

Tests:
- Unit test: export a Linear model, verify ONNX file is valid protobuf
- Unit test: exported model contains correct op types and shapes
- Integration test (if onnxruntime-rs available): run exported model and compare outputs
- Edge case: export model with dynamic batch size","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:10.592448782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:22.990921829Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankentorch-iey","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-2er","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:22.990453482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankentorch-igu","title":"Add adversarial/fuzz test coverage for high-risk paths (spec Gate G4)","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §5 requires 'adversarial fixtures and fuzz/property suites for high-risk entry points' and §18 defines Gate G4 (adversarial + property tests) as release-blocking.

Current state: ft-serialize has 5 proptest properties. ft-core has none. ft-api, ft-autograd, ft-kernel-cpu have none. The spec's Security Threat Matrix (§16) defines 8 threat categories that need adversarial coverage.

Implementation plan:
1. Add proptest properties for ft-autograd:
   - Random computation graphs maintain gradient correctness (numerical gradient check)
   - Backward on disconnected subgraphs doesn't corrupt other gradients
   - In-place mutation version tracking prevents stale gradient reads
2. Add proptest properties for ft-kernel-cpu:
   - Broadcasting produces correct shapes for random input shapes
   - Reduction ops produce correct results for random input tensors (cross-check against naive impl)
   - Matmul associativity: (A*B)*C matches A*(B*C) within tolerance
3. Add proptest properties for ft-api:
   - Random tensor ops maintain shape consistency (output shape matches PyTorch rules)
   - save_state_dict -> load_state_dict round-trip preserves all values for random state dicts
4. Add adversarial fixture tests for ft-serialize:
   - Fuzzed JSON payloads don't cause panics (catch_unwind + verify Error returned)
   - Fuzzed binary payloads don't cause panics
   - Oversized shape dimensions don't cause OOM (our recent checked_mul fix)
5. Add adversarial tests for ft-nn:
   - NaN/Inf inputs to forward() produce NaN/Inf output (not panic)
   - Zero-sized batch inputs handled gracefully

Tests: The beads ARE the tests. Each property test should use proptest with 256+ cases.
Success criterion: cargo test --workspace --all-features passes with all new property tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:58.165045573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:24:26.092380322Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:24:26.092126241Z","close_reason":"Added 8 property-based tests: 4 in ft-autograd (add/mul gradient correctness, backward determinism, relu gradient indicator) + 4 in ft-kernel-cpu (neg self-inverse, exp/log round-trip, sum vs naive, softmax sums-to-one). All pass with proptest 256+ cases. Added proptest dev-dependency to ft-kernel-cpu.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankentorch-iha","title":"Add mixed precision support (torch.amp equivalent)","description":"torch.cuda.amp (Automatic Mixed Precision) is used in virtually every modern training pipeline. Even on CPU, F16/BF16 mixed precision can be useful for inference and for testing AMP workflows before moving to GPU.

Since FrankenTorch already supports F16 and BF16 dtypes in ft-core, the infrastructure exists — what's missing is the autocast and grad scaling machinery.

Implementation plan:
1. Add an AutocastContext to ft-api that tracks whether autocast is active and the target dtype
2. Add session.autocast_enter(dtype) / session.autocast_exit() (matching no_grad pattern)
3. Add session.with_autocast(dtype, closure) panic-safe wrapper
4. When autocast is active: matmul, linear, conv operations auto-downcast inputs to the target dtype (F16/BF16), then upcast output back to F32
5. Add a GradScaler struct in ft-optim that:
   a. Scales loss by a factor before backward (prevents F16 underflow)
   b. Unscales gradients before optimizer step
   c. Checks for inf/nan in gradients, skipping the update if found
   d. Adjusts scale factor dynamically based on overflow frequency

Key: The autocast policy (which ops to cast) should match PyTorch's known-safe list — matmul, linear, conv are safe; softmax, layernorm, loss functions should stay in F32.

Tests:
- Unit test: autocast context enters/exits correctly, respects nesting
- Unit test: matmul inside autocast uses F16, outside uses F32
- Unit test: GradScaler scales loss and unscales gradients
- Unit test: GradScaler skips update on inf gradients
- E2E test: train a linear model with autocast + GradScaler, verify convergence
- Panic-safety: with_autocast restores state on unwind","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:09.970716552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:09.970716552Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-iha","title":"Add mixed precision support (torch.amp equivalent)","description":"torch.cuda.amp (Automatic Mixed Precision) is used in virtually every modern training pipeline. Even on CPU, F16/BF16 mixed precision can be useful for inference and for testing AMP workflows before moving to GPU.

Since FrankenTorch already supports F16 and BF16 dtypes in ft-core, the infrastructure exists — what's missing is the autocast and grad scaling machinery.

Implementation plan:
1. Add an AutocastContext to ft-api that tracks whether autocast is active and the target dtype
2. Add session.autocast_enter(dtype) / session.autocast_exit() (matching no_grad pattern)
3. Add session.with_autocast(dtype, closure) panic-safe wrapper
4. When autocast is active: matmul, linear, conv operations auto-downcast inputs to the target dtype (F16/BF16), then upcast output back to F32
5. Add a GradScaler struct in ft-optim that:
   a. Scales loss by a factor before backward (prevents F16 underflow)
   b. Unscales gradients before optimizer step
   c. Checks for inf/nan in gradients, skipping the update if found
   d. Adjusts scale factor dynamically based on overflow frequency

Key: The autocast policy (which ops to cast) should match PyTorch's known-safe list — matmul, linear, conv are safe; softmax, layernorm, loss functions should stay in F32.

Tests:
- Unit test: autocast context enters/exits correctly, respects nesting
- Unit test: matmul inside autocast uses F16, outside uses F32
- Unit test: GradScaler scales loss and unscales gradients
- Unit test: GradScaler skips update on inf gradients
- E2E test: train a linear model with autocast + GradScaler, verify convergence
- Panic-safety: with_autocast restores state on unwind","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:09.970716552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T23:42:34.450624484Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T23:42:34.450297625Z","close_reason":"Implemented mixed precision support: (1) ft-api: autocast_enter/exit/with_autocast/maybe_autocast with stack-based RAII context, panic-safe; (2) ft-optim: GradScaler with scale_loss/step/update + dynamic backoff/growth, overflow detection, skip-on-inf semantics; (3) ft-autograd: TensorBackwardReport::tensor_gradients_iter() and scaled_clone() helpers. 8 autocast tests + 7 GradScaler tests = 15 new tests. Note: F16/BF16 conversion paths gated until ft-autograd to_dtype supports them; F32/F64 down-casting works.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-iiv","title":"Reject impossible multinomial draws without replacement","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-30T02:30:49.283027420Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-30T02:34:11.910610518Z","closed_at":"2026-03-30T02:34:11.910332993Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankentorch-ioo","title":"Add CI pipeline with G1-G5 quality gates from spec","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §18 defines 5 CI gates that are release-blocking. NONE of them exist:

| Gate | Name | Blocking | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| G1 | format + lint | yes | lint report |
| G2 | unit + integration | yes | junit report |
| G3 | differential + gradient checks | yes | parity report + grad report |
| G4 | adversarial + property tests | yes | counterexample corpus |
| G5 | benchmark regression | yes | baseline delta report |

Implementation:
1. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with 5 jobs mapping to G1-G5
2. G1: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings
3. G2: cargo test --workspace --all-features (with junit output via cargo-nextest or similar)
4. G3: cargo test -p ft-conformance (differential fixtures), cargo test -p ft-autograd (gradient checks)
5. G4: cargo test --workspace --all-features (proptest runs as part of normal test suite)
6. G5: cargo run -p ft-conformance --bin run_perf_slos (depends on frankentorch-vws being done first)

Considerations:
- GitHub Actions runners may be slow for full test suite; consider splitting into parallel jobs
- G5 (benchmark regression) needs baseline artifacts committed to repo for comparison
- Need to use nightly toolchain (rust-toolchain.toml already specifies this)

Tests:
- The CI pipeline IS the test — verify it by pushing a PR and checking all 5 gates pass
- Ensure the pipeline also runs on the 'main' branch (not just PRs)
- Add a badge to README.md showing CI status","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:38.761474655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:59.352295686Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankentorch-ioo","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-3cp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:59.351795619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankentorch-ioo","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-vws","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:22.900747037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

| G3 | differential + gradient checks | yes | parity report + grad report |
| G4 | adversarial + property tests | yes | counterexample corpus |
| G5 | benchmark regression | yes | baseline delta report |

2. G1: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings
3. G2: cargo test --workspace --all-features (with junit output via cargo-nextest or similar)

6. G5: cargo run -p ft-conformance --bin run_perf_slos (depends on frankentorch-vws being done first)

Considerations:

Tests:

- Ensure the pipeline also runs on the 'main' branch (not just PRs)
- Add a badge to README.md showing CI status","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:38.761474655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T23:43:08.208191542Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T23:43:08.207936639Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: .github/workflows/phase2c_reliability_gates.yml has G1 (fmt/clippy) + G2 (unit/property) + G3 (differential) + G4 (e2e/forensics) + G5 (perf) + G6 (schema) + G7 (RaptorQ) + G8 (readiness). Exceeds spec's G1-G5 requirement. Uses rch offload when available, falls back to local. Has structured failure reporting with gate_id, replay commands, artifact refs, and reason codes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankentorch-ioo","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-3cp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:59.351795619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankentorch-ioo","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-vws","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:22.900747037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankentorch-j33","title":"ft-core: fail closed on integer/bool storage span overflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:18:33.514330249Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:21:38.818312755Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T16:21:38.818066108Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-jhi","title":"Make Optimizer::set_momentum return Result instead of silent no-op","description":"## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found that `Optimizer::set_momentum(&mut self, _momentum: f64)` in ft-optim/src/lib.rs:111 has an empty body `{}`. This is the ONLY empty function body in the entire codebase.

The paired `get_momentum()` default returns `None`, which is correct. But `set_momentum()` silently discards the value with no feedback — a caller setting momentum on an Adagrad optimizer (which does not support momentum) gets no error and no indication that their intent was ignored.

## Problem
Silent data loss violates the principle of least surprise. In PyTorch, calling an unsupported setter is either a no-op with a warning or raises an error depending on the optimizer. FrankenTorch should be at least as explicit.

## Plan
1. Change signature to `fn set_momentum(&mut self, _momentum: f64) -> Result<(), AutogradError>`
2. Default implementation returns `Err(optimizer_hparam_error("this optimizer does not support momentum"))`
3. SGD (and any other momentum-supporting optimizer) overrides to actually set the value and return `Ok(())`
4. Update all callers to handle the Result
5. Add a test verifying that non-momentum optimizers reject set_momentum

## Acceptance Criteria
- `set_momentum` on Adagrad/RMSprop/RAdam returns descriptive Err
- `set_momentum` on SGD succeeds
- All existing optimizer tests pass
- Zero empty function bodies remain in the codebase

## Dependencies
None — this is an independent API change within ft-optim.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:22:34.711976324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:45:29.961692699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T23:45:29.961433909Z","close_reason":"Optimizer::set_momentum now returns an explicit unsupported-feature error by default, SGD implements Result-based momentum updates, OneCycleLR handles failures visibly, and ft-optim tests plus workspace check/clippy passed via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-jin","title":"Replace partial_cmp().unwrap() with NaN-safe total ordering in kernel sorting","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T02:20:44.502026815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T02:22:41.960154418Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T02:22:41.959815497Z","close_reason":"Replaced partial_cmp().unwrap() with total_cmp() in eigh and SVD sorting. Prevents panic on NaN eigenvalues/singular values.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-ndx","title":"Sparse COO to_dense should sum duplicate indices","description":"SparseCOOTensor::to_dense overwrote duplicate indices instead of summing; fix to accumulate duplicates (including dense-block case) and add regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T22:14:44.023609749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T22:19:34.225190591Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T22:19:34.224868191Z","close_reason":"Accumulate duplicate COO entries in to_dense","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-oaw","title":"Stabilize rch remote sync/artifact retrieval under volatile workspace churn","notes":"Investigated repo-local rch causes. Findings: no project .rch/config.toml, no .cargo/config.toml, no legacy_pytorch_code tree in this checkout, no local .venv*, and no FrankenTorch-specific target-dir override. Global rch config already excludes target/, target_*/, target-*/, and .beads/. Remaining intermittent rsync/file-vanished noise appears daemon/artifact-sync side rather than a safe repo-local misconfiguration to edit here.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:33:36.582627630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:38:47.775805569Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:38:47.775198931Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-plj","title":"Add gradient checkpointing (torch.utils.checkpoint equivalent)","description":"torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint() allows trading compute for memory by not saving intermediate activations during forward, then recomputing them during backward. This is essential for training large models that don't fit in memory.

Implementation plan:
1. Add a session.checkpoint(function, inputs) method that:
   a. Runs the function in no_grad context (saves no intermediates)
   b. Records a special CheckpointedSegment node on the autograd tape
   c. During backward: re-runs the function WITH grad enabled to recompute activations, then computes gradients normally
2. The key invariant: gradients must be identical whether checkpoint is used or not (just slower with lower memory)

Design considerations:
- Must work with the existing TensorTape backward scheduler
- Must properly handle nested checkpointing
- Must respect the DAC contract (deterministic replay must still work)
- Re-execution during backward must produce identical activations (deterministic PRNG state needs saving)

Tests:
- Unit test: simple function with checkpoint vs without — gradients must match exactly
- Unit test: nested checkpointing (checkpoint inside checkpoint)
- Unit test: checkpoint respects manual_seed for reproducibility
- E2E test: train a deep network with and without checkpointing — final loss should be identical
- Memory test: verify peak memory is lower with checkpointing (if measurable)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:52.757885399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:36:14.882158581Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:36:14.881802397Z","close_reason":"Implemented session.checkpoint() that trades compute for memory. Runs function in no_grad (no intermediates saved), registers backward node via tensor_apply_function that re-executes the function with grad enabled during backward. 3 tests: correct forward output, gradient equivalence with non-checkpointed, multiple inputs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-t33","title":"Document trait default method contracts with doc comments","description":"## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found 8 trait default methods that return trivial values. All are architecturally correct but several lack doc comments explaining WHY the default is what it is and WHEN implementors should override.

Prerequisite beads completed:
- frankentorch-jhi (CLOSED): Changed Optimizer::set_momentum to return Result
- frankentorch-tk5 (CLOSED): Added module_type to ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported, using std::any::type_name::<Self>() in the trait default

## Affected methods (8 total)

### Module trait (ft-nn/src/lib.rs)
Current state (verified 2026-04-03):

1. `named_parameters_own()` -> Vec::new()
   - Has doc: "Return this module's own named parameters (non-recursive)." + explanation about containers
   - NEEDS: "# Default behavior" and "# When to override" sections

2. `named_parameter_slots_own()` -> Vec::new()
   - Has doc: "Return dynamically-registered own parameters, including optional slots."
   - NEEDS: default/override guidance

3. `named_buffer_slots_own()` -> Vec::new()
   - Has doc: "Return own registered buffers, including persistence metadata."
   - NEEDS: default/override guidance

4. `named_children()` -> Vec::new()
   - Has doc: "Return direct child sub-modules with their local names." + naming conventions
   - NEEDS: explicit note that leaf modules should keep the default

5. `register_parameter()` -> Err(Unsupported{module_type, operation})
   - Has doc: "Register (or replace) a named parameter slot."
   - NEEDS: note about default returning Unsupported with module_type context, and when to override

6. `register_buffer()` -> Err(Unsupported{module_type, operation})
   - Has doc: "Register (or replace) a named buffer slot."
   - NEEDS: same as register_parameter

### Optimizer trait (ft-optim/src/lib.rs)
Current state (verified 2026-04-03):

7. `get_momentum()` -> None
   - Has doc: "Return momentum if the optimizer exposes one."
   - NEEDS: note that None means the optimizer has no momentum concept, and override when momentum exists (e.g., SGD)

8. `set_momentum()` -> Err(unsupported)
   - Has doc: "Set momentum for optimizers that expose it."
   - NEEDS: note about the Result return, when to override, and that non-momentum optimizers return Err

## Doc comment enhancement template
For EACH method, ENHANCE (do not replace) the existing doc comment by appending:

```rust
///
/// # Default behavior
/// Returns [specific value]. This is correct for [category of types] because [reason].
///
/// # When to override
/// Override this when [condition]. Example: [`Linear`] overrides this to [what it does].
```

Do NOT delete or modify existing doc comment text — only append the new sections.

## Plan
1. Read ft-nn/src/lib.rs and ft-optim/src/lib.rs to find exact current line numbers
2. For each of the 8 methods, append the default/override sections to the existing doc comment
3. Verify: `rch exec -- cargo doc -p ft-nn -p ft-optim --no-deps 2>&1` — zero warnings
4. Verify: `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ft-nn -p ft-optim -- -D warnings` — clean
5. Verify: `git diff` shows ONLY doc comment changes, zero logic modifications

## Testing requirements

### Automated verification
- `rch exec -- cargo doc -p ft-nn -p ft-optim --no-deps` — zero warnings
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn -p ft-optim` — all existing tests pass (doc changes can't break tests, but verify)
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ft-nn -p ft-optim --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean

### Manual verification (implementing agent must confirm)
- [ ] All 8 methods have "# Default behavior" section
- [ ] All 8 methods have "# When to override" section
- [ ] No method signature or logic was changed
- [ ] Existing doc text was preserved (only appended to)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 8 methods have enhanced doc comments with default behavior + override guidance
- cargo doc clean
- cargo test clean
- cargo clippy clean
- git diff shows only comment additions

## Dependencies
- frankentorch-tk5 (CLOSED) — added module_type to Unsupported variant, docs must reflect this
- frankentorch-jhi (CLOSED) — changed set_momentum signature, docs must reflect Result return

## Reasoning
Future auditors (human or AI) encountering Vec::new() defaults may incorrectly flag them as stubs. Explicit "# Default behavior" sections prevent this and serve as self-documenting contracts.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:23:17.607354829Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:05:57.884182107Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:05:57.884004772Z","close_reason":"Expanded default-trait docs for ft-nn Module traversal/registration hooks and ft-optim momentum hooks, then verified cargo doc/check/clippy via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankentorch-t33","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-tk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:46.661582666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"frankentorch-tbb","title":"Pre-existing test failure: custom_function_inside_no_grad_does_not_record_on_tape","description":"Pre-existing failure from commit ec29fb5 (feat(autograd,conformance): add custom function grad-disable tests). Test asserts s.tensor_grad_fn(y).unwrap().is_none() but the assertion fails. This is unrelated to gradient checkpointing or mixed precision work — verified by stashing recent changes and reproducing the failure.

Test location: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs:26666

The test creates a tensor_apply_function inside no_grad context and expects:
1. tensor_requires_grad(y) returns false ✓ (this part passes)
2. tensor_grad_fn(y) returns None ✗ (this part fails)
3. tensor_backward(y) returns TensorRootDoesNotRequireGrad

The fix likely requires ensuring tensor_apply_function records the grad_fn as None when grad is disabled in the parent session.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-08T23:42:24.142360204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T23:45:04.190471381Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T23:45:04.190222720Z","close_reason":"Fixed: TensorTape::apply_function now records the output as a Leaf node (not CustomFunction) when grad is disabled or no input requires grad. The pre-existing test now passes, all 8 custom_function tests pass, all 309 ft-autograd tests pass, and gradient checkpointing still works correctly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-tk5","title":"Enrich Module register_parameter/register_buffer errors with module type context","description":"## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found that Module::register_parameter() and Module::register_buffer() default implementations (ft-nn/src/lib.rs) return `Err(ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported { operation: "register_parameter" })`.

The error tells you WHAT operation failed but not WHICH module rejected it. When debugging a deep Sequential model with 50+ layers, "register_parameter unsupported" gives no clue about which layer is the problem.

## Current error type
```rust
pub enum ModuleRegistrationError {
    InvalidName { kind: &'static str },
    NameConflict { name: String },
    Unsupported { operation: &'static str },
}
```

## Design decision: How to get the module type name in a trait default

The trait default method signature is `fn register_parameter(&mut self, ...)`. We CANNOT call `std::any::type_name::<Self>()` because the default method has `Self: ?Sized` (it's on `dyn Module`).

**Solution**: Add a new required method to the Module trait:
```rust
fn module_type_name(&self) -> &'static str;
```
This is cheap (one &'static str per impl), gives correct type info even through dyn dispatch, and follows the same pattern PyTorch uses with `_get_name()`.

Each module implementation returns its name: "Linear", "Conv2d", "Sequential", etc.

The default for register_parameter/register_buffer then becomes:
```rust
fn register_parameter(...) -> Result<(), ModuleRegistrationError> {
    Err(ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported {
        operation: "register_parameter",
        module_type: self.module_type_name(),
    })
}
```

## Plan
1. Add `module_type_name(&self) -> &'static str` to the Module trait (no default — every impl must provide it)
2. Add `module_type: &'static str` field to `ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported`
3. Update the Display impl to print "{module_type} does not support {operation}"
4. Update default register_parameter/register_buffer to include module_type
5. Add `module_type_name()` to ALL existing Module implementations (~40+ structs):
   - Linear -> "Linear", Conv1d -> "Conv1d", Sequential -> "Sequential", etc.
6. Update any tests that construct or match ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported

## Testing requirements

### Unit tests (in ft-nn #[cfg(test)])

#### test_unsupported_register_includes_module_type
- Call register_parameter on a module that doesn't override it (e.g., ReLU)
- Assert the error message contains "ReLU"
- Assert the error message contains "register_parameter"

#### test_unsupported_register_buffer_includes_module_type
- Same for register_buffer on a leaf module

#### test_all_modules_have_type_name
- For each major module type (Linear, Conv1d, Conv2d, LSTM, Sequential, etc.):
  - Construct an instance
  - Assert module_type_name() returns a non-empty string
  - Assert module_type_name() does NOT contain "Module" alone (should be specific)

#### test_module_type_name_matches_struct_name
- For a selection of modules, verify that module_type_name() matches the Rust struct name
- E.g., Linear::module_type_name() == "Linear"

#### test_sequential_register_parameter_succeeds
- Verify that Sequential (which DOES support register_parameter) still works correctly
- Construct a Sequential, register a parameter, verify it appears in named_parameter_slots_own()

### E2E validation
- Run `rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn` — all existing + new tests pass
- Run `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ft-nn --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean

## Acceptance Criteria
- Error messages include the module type name (e.g., "ReLU does not support register_parameter")
- ALL Module implementations have module_type_name()
- 5+ new tests covering the enriched error paths
- Existing tests still pass
- Clippy clean

## Risk
Medium — this adds a required trait method to ~40 implementations. Use grep to find all `impl Module for` blocks. Each just needs one line added. Can be parallelized with subagents.

## Dependencies
None — isolated change within ft-nn.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:23:05.244308597Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T00:26:31.066854625Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T00:26:31.066605804Z","close_reason":"ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported now carries module_type context, default Module registration methods report std::any::type_name::<Self>(), and ft-nn test plus workspace check/clippy passed via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"frankentorch-vws","title":"Validate performance SLOs from spec against actual measurements","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §17 defines 8 performance budgets that have NEVER been measured:

| Path | Workload | Budget |
|---|---|---|
| elementwise forward | 100M elements | p95 <= 200 ms |
| backward pass | representative autograd graphs | p95 <= 1.35x forward baseline |
| matmul core | medium dense matrices | p95 <= 240 ms |
| optimizer step | 10M params Adam | p95 <= 130 ms |
| checkpoint save/load | 2 GB model state | throughput >= 350 MB/s |
| memory footprint | training step profile | peak RSS regression <= +8% |
| allocation churn | backward-heavy trace | alloc count regression <= +10% |
| tail stability | benchmark families | p99 regression <= +7% |

The spec's M4 milestone (Hardening) requires performance gates to pass. Currently we have exactly one benchmark (scalar DAC microbench, p95=4698ns) which doesn't cover any of the 8 SLOs.

Implementation plan:
1. Create a benchmark binary in ft-conformance (or a new crate) that runs each of the 8 workloads
2. For each: run 100 iterations, compute p50/p95/p99, compare against the budget
3. Emit structured JSON results to artifacts/phase2c/performance/
4. Create a gate script that reads the JSON and fails if any SLO is exceeded
5. Document whether FrankenTorch meets, exceeds, or fails each budget

Key consideration: We're CPU-only and pure Rust with no BLAS — we may not meet matmul or elementwise budgets for 100M elements. That's OK — the goal is to MEASURE and know where we stand, then document realistic budgets.

Tests:
- Benchmark binary should be runnable via: rch exec -- cargo run -p ft-conformance --bin run_perf_slos
- JSON output should be machine-readable and diffable
- Gate script should exit 0 (pass) or 1 (fail) with human-readable report","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:24.426519762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:07:53.847148434Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-vws","title":"Validate performance SLOs from spec against actual measurements","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §17 defines 8 performance budgets that have NEVER been measured:

| Path | Workload | Budget |
|---|---|---|
| elementwise forward | 100M elements | p95 <= 200 ms |
| backward pass | representative autograd graphs | p95 <= 1.35x forward baseline |
| matmul core | medium dense matrices | p95 <= 240 ms |
| optimizer step | 10M params Adam | p95 <= 130 ms |
| checkpoint save/load | 2 GB model state | throughput >= 350 MB/s |
| memory footprint | training step profile | peak RSS regression <= +8% |
| allocation churn | backward-heavy trace | alloc count regression <= +10% |
| tail stability | benchmark families | p99 regression <= +7% |

The spec's M4 milestone (Hardening) requires performance gates to pass. Currently we have exactly one benchmark (scalar DAC microbench, p95=4698ns) which doesn't cover any of the 8 SLOs.

Implementation plan:
1. Create a benchmark binary in ft-conformance (or a new crate) that runs each of the 8 workloads
2. For each: run 100 iterations, compute p50/p95/p99, compare against the budget
3. Emit structured JSON results to artifacts/phase2c/performance/
4. Create a gate script that reads the JSON and fails if any SLO is exceeded
5. Document whether FrankenTorch meets, exceeds, or fails each budget

Key consideration: We're CPU-only and pure Rust with no BLAS — we may not meet matmul or elementwise budgets for 100M elements. That's OK — the goal is to MEASURE and know where we stand, then document realistic budgets.

Tests:
- Benchmark binary should be runnable via: rch exec -- cargo run -p ft-conformance --bin run_perf_slos
- JSON output should be machine-readable and diffable
- Gate script should exit 0 (pass) or 1 (fail) with human-readable report","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:24.426519762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:59.370070209Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:59.369819234Z","close_reason":"Implemented perf-SLO runner/gate, validated smoke path, and captured a verified single-sample full-profile measurement with documented direct-budget failures plus baseline-required regression budgets; remaining baseline/CI handoff moved to frankentorch-3cp.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"frankentorch-wze","title":"Escalate blocked_issues_cache import-path page leak and preserve safe tracker workflow","description":"Follow-up to frankentorch-xsp.

Investigation isolated the integrity regression to the import path: explicit `br sync --import-only` and any command that auto-imports current JSONL into a clean vacuumed DB. The stable signature is blocked_issues_cache churn: old blocked-cache table/index pages become `PRAGMA integrity_check` `never used` pages after import/rebuild.

Safe on a clean vacuumed DB: `br info`, `br doctor`, `br sync --status`, and commands with `--no-auto-import`.

Artifacts:
- artifacts/beads_recovery/FRANKENTORCH_XSP_IMPORT_PATH_REPORT_20260407.md
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_probe_20260407T025948Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_import_focus_20260407T030054Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_repeat_import_20260407T030112Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_sequence_20260407T030136Z/results.json

Next work:
1. Escalate the import-path / blocked-cache page leak upstream in beads_rust or frankensqlite.
2. Preserve a local recovery playbook that warns agents to avoid auto-import on freshly vacuumed recovery DBs until the upstream fix lands.
3. Re-validate the workaround after the upstream fix by repeating the xsp probe matrix.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:04:23.139752494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:05:03.896531775Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankentorch-0tw","title":"Add sparse tensor storage and basic sparse ops","description":"## Progress

### Completed
1. SparseCOOTensor in ft-core with indices [sparse_dim, nnz], values [nnz, *dense_dims], coalesced flag
2. SparseCSRTensor in ft-core with crow_indices, col_indices, values
3. SparseTensorError enum with comprehensive validation errors
4. AutogradError::SparseTensor variant for error propagation
5. ft-api sparse tensor methods
6. ft-kernel-cpu sparse operations
7. GradientValue enum (Dense/Sparse) in ft-autograd for unified gradient representation
8. sparse_gradients field in TensorBackwardReport for sparse gradient storage
9. Embedding.sparse field in ft-nn with with_options(sparse) constructor
10. SparseAdam now checks report for sparse gradients and iterates efficiently over COO entries
11. (this commit) Wired sparse backward path in IndexSelect when source tensor is marked sparse
12. (this commit) Added Embedding+SparseAdam roundtrip test
